an10.48:1.1: “There are these ten things that a person gone forth should reflect on often.
an10.48:2.1: “‘I have become casteless’: A person gone forth should often reflect on this.
an10.48:4.1: “‘My behavior should be different (from that of householders)’ …
an10.48:5.1: “‘Can I fault myself with regard to my virtue?’…
an10.48:6.1: “‘Can my observant fellows in the holy life, on close examination, fault me with regard to my virtue?’ …
an10.48:7.1: “‘I will grow different, separate from all that is dear & appealing to me’ …
an10.48:8.1: “‘I am the owner of actions [kamma], heir to actions, born of actions, related through actions, and have actions as my arbitrator.
an10.48:9.1: Whatever I do, for good or for evil, to that will I fall heir’ …
an10.48:10.1: “‘What am I becoming as the days & nights fly past?’ …
an10.48:11.1: “‘Do I delight in an empty dwelling?’ …
an10.48:12.1: “‘Have I attained a superior human attainment, a truly noble distinction of knowledge & vision, such that—when my companions in the holy life question me in the last days of my life—I won’t feel abashed?’: A person gone forth should often reflect on this.
an10.48:13.1: “These are the ten things that a person gone forth should reflect on often.”
